input.stepLabel: Initiate refund-to-source on completed UPI-mandate payment — expect 201 + refundId
input.errorMessage: PaymentGatewayError: UPI mandate refund-to-source returned HTTP 422 — 'mandate_status: REVOKED'. Original mandate was active at purchase time (2026-04-15) but has been revoked by user (revoked_at: 2026-05-10). Gateway response: { errorCode: 'MANDATE_REVOKED', message: 'Cannot refund to revoked mandate. Use alternate refund channel.' }
input.stackTrace:
PaymentGatewayError at upi-mandate.client.ts:127 refundToMandate()
  at payment-gateway.service.ts:84 dispatchRefund()
  at refund.service.ts:201 executeRefund()
kbContext: UPI mandates can be revoked by users at any time via the UPI app. Once revoked, the refund-to-source channel is permanently closed for that mandate; refunds must fall back to a different channel (bank transfer or wallet credit).
The refund service should pre-check mandate status before calling refundToMandate(); if status != ACTIVE, fall back to bank-transfer refund channel.
Iksula refund-channel fallback policy: UPI mandate revoked → try saved bank account on file → try Iksula wallet credit (always last resort).

groundTruth.rootCauseDetail: Refund service does not pre-check UPI mandate status before calling refundToMandate(). When the user revokes the mandate post-purchase, the refund attempt 422s. Fix: in refund.service.ts:201 executeRefund(), look up mandate status first; if revoked, route to fallback channel (bank-transfer per KB policy).
groundTruth.notesForEval: Top-2 hit on payment-gateway or code-bug. payment-gateway captures the integration aspect (gateway returned a specific failure mode we didn't handle); code-bug captures the missing pre-check logic. Either label correct. Sherlock should ideally cite refund.service.ts:201 + the KB chunk about mandate revocation handling.
